text: When working on a project with Git, it's common to use feature branches. A feature branch allows you to work on a new function or fix without affecting the main codebase, often called the main branch. Once your work is complete, you can merge it back into the main branch. Before merging, many teams use pull requests to review the code changes. This helps catch errors and improve quality. Overall, this version control workflow keeps the project organised, reduces conflicts, and makes collaboration smoother for everyone involved.
